column1: Technical Specifications FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF A MODULE FOR COLLECTING BROWSER COOKIES
The module should collect cookies of the main browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Internet Explorer, Microsoft Edge) and send them via DPOST protocol to the server.
- designed in accordance with the rules of module development
- the only module config is the list of DPOST pads
- works both on behalf of the user and SYSTEM
Only cookies with no expiration date, or not yet expired in time, are collected.
If the module is running from SYSTEM, it enumerates all non-system users and collects cookies for each of them.
If the module is running from a regular user, it collects cookies only for that user.
3. When finished, the module should give a WantRelease event (see "module_HOWTO") to unload from memory
The collected cookies are sent until a positive response is received from the pads.
If there is no communication with the pads, the module goes to sleep for half an hour, after which it retries.
The module sends the following events:
- "Cookies grabbed from <browser name> for user <username>" on collection success
- "No cookies from <browser name> for user <username>" if the given browser has no cookies
- "Cookies sent to server" on success of sending to server DPOST
- "Cookies send failure: servers unavailable" if there are no DPOST servers available.
In this module you can use C++ STL (std::string, containers) in a limited way.
It is forbidden to use std::mutex and synchronization primitives - for this purpose you can use only
7. Obfuscate strings with Andrivet library (attached, see _
Obfuscate system calls with GetApi.h library.
Be careful, obfuscation of syscalls may cause crashes.
The module must have two versions - x32- and x64-bit.
In the combat build, lines should be obfuscated to the maximum and all debugging output should be disabled.
The module must have a debug version.
Debug output should be output to c:/temp/cookies.log (the path to the log is configurable in the macro).
The project must have a config.h file (the name is not important, the essence is important - all global settings - paths, macros-switches for conditional compilation, etc.).
The module should work on all modern versions of Windows.
The minimum supported Windows version is Windows XP (if it is impossible - Windows Vista).
See the document "DPOST Password Rob TOR".
Regarding this document, the format of sending has been changed:
created - date-time of creation, Unix time
